title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Why Women Who Run the World Should Invest in ULIPs?
Women are increasingly breaking the glass ceiling and taking control of their careers. Besides running their households, women are also running companies, businesses, and even the Government.
In the seventh edition of a report titled ‘Women in the Boardroom’ published by Deloitte Global, women hold 17.1% of seats on the Board of Directors in India. The number grew by 9.4% from 2014. A considerable increase1!
Women are natural leaders. They make what they handle, whether it is their household or their careers. For such career-oriented women, managing their financial portfolios is also important so that they can make their money work as hard as they do. A unit-linked life insurance policy can be one such beneficial addition that can give women multiple benefits.
Let’s have a look at the ULIP benefits that make them a good addition to women’s financial planning:
Investment-cum-insurance plans
A ULIP is a unique life insurance policy that combines the benefits of insurance protection and investment returns in one. The plan offers life insurance coverage against premature death. If the insured passes away during the policy tenure, the ULIP pays a guaranteed death benefit to the bereaved family.
Moreover, the investment angle of the plan makes it advantageous. You can invest in the capital market through diversified and professionally managed fund portfolios. These portfolios allow you to generate returns so that you can create a suitable corpus for your goals.
Attractive returns
As mentioned above, ULIPs invest the invested premium in the market. After that, as the market performs, your investment grows. The capital market is dynamic and has the potential to generate attractive returns on your investment if you invest with a long-term perspective. Moreover, since the returns are linked to market performance, they are adjusted for inflation too. You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the returns that you can get on your investment.
Thus, you can maximise your wealth and build up a suitable corpus.
Goal-oriented savings
With the different types of ULIPs, women can create an earmarked corpus for each of their financial goals. For instance –
Child ULIPs help mothers plan a secured corpus for their child’s future.
Whole life ULIPs help women to secure their family’s financial future
Pension ULIPs help women create a retirement corpus so that they are financially independent even after they retire.
Thus, with goal-oriented savings, women can create a diversified and earmarked portfolio for their financial obligations.
Flexible benefits
Another ULIP benefit is its flexibility. With ULIPs, women can take control of their finances. They can invest in different types of funds based on their risk appetite and investment strategy. They can switch the funds whenever they need to without any restrictions. Then there is the availability of partial withdrawals that allow women to withdraw from their ULIP if they need financial assistance during the policy term.
Other flexible benefits include top-up premiums, premium redirection, settlement options, etc. ULIPs, thus, empower women to handle their finances in ways that they deem fit.
Tax savings⁴:
Lastly, the tax-saving benefit of a ULIP helps women reduce their tax liabilities and also creates a tax-efficient saving corpus. Women can enjoy tax deductions under Section 80C2 on the premiums that they pay for the ULIP. Switching and partial withdrawals do not attract tax. The death benefit is completely tax-free too. Moreover, if you have bought the ULIP on or after 1st February 2021 and the aggregate premium that you paid is within Rs.2.5 lakhs3, you can also enjoy tax exemption on the maturity benefit. The maturity benefit is allowed as a tax-free income under Section 10(10D).
With women revolutionising the world with their contribution, it's time to revolutionise their finances too. Given the benefits that ULIPs offer, they can prove to be a useful addition to a woman’s financial portfolio and help her achieve financial independence.
So, understand what ULIPs are, use the ULIP calculator to estimate the expected returns, and then invest in a ULIP to create an adequate corpus for your goals.
